name: scikit-bio
description: Biological data toolkit. Sequence analysis, alignments, phylogenetic trees, diversity metrics (alpha/beta, UniFrac), ordination (PCoA), PERMANOVA, FASTA/Newick I/O, for microbiome analysis.
capabilities: Guide users through reading and writing FASTA, FASTQ, GenBank, and Newick file formats
Demonstrate pairwise and multiple sequence alignment techniques using dynamic programming
Show how to calculate alpha diversity metrics including Shannon, Simpson, and Faith Phylogenetic Diversity
Explain beta diversity calculations using Bray-Curtis, Jaccard, and UniFrac distances
Provide examples of phylogenetic tree construction with UPGMA, Neighbor Joining, and tree comparison methods
Demonstrate statistical testing with PERMANOVA, ANOSIM, Mantel tests and ordination analysis
author: davila7
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown reference material for the scikit-bio Python bioinformatics library. All 137 static findings are false positives from Python code examples within markdown documentation. No executable code, network calls, or system access present.
valueStatement: Biological research requires specialized tools for DNA sequence analysis, phylogenetic trees, and diversity metrics. This skill provides guidance for using scikit-bio to process sequences, calculate alpha and beta diversity, and perform statistical tests on microbiome data.
userTitle: Analyze biological sequences and microbiome diversity
limitations: Provides documentation and guidance only, does not execute code or install libraries
Users must install scikit-bio separately using pip or conda package managers
Does not include tools for sequence assembly, variant calling, or genome annotation
Requires user to have Python programming knowledge to implement examples

useCases.title: Analyze microbial community composition
useCases.description: Calculate diversity metrics from OTU tables, perform PCoA ordination, and test group differences with PERMANOVA statistical tests.

useCases.title: Process DNA sequencing data
useCases.description: Read FASTQ files, filter sequences by quality scores, find motifs, translate to proteins, and export results.

useCases.title: Build and compare phylogenetic trees
useCases.description: Construct trees from distance matrices, calculate patristic distances, and compare tree topologies using Robinson-Foulds metrics.

promptTemplates.title: Read DNA Sequence
promptTemplates.scenario: Load and analyze a FASTA file
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use scikit-bio to read a DNA sequence from a FASTA file, get its reverse complement, transcribe it to RNA, and find all ATG start codon positions.

promptTemplates.title: Calculate Alpha Diversity
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me use scikit-bio to calculate Shannon diversity, Simpson index, and observed OTU counts from a sample-by-feature abundance matrix.

promptTemplates.title: Run PERMANOVA Test
promptTemplates.scenario: Test if sample groups differ significantly
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through running a PERMANOVA test in scikit-bio using a beta diversity distance matrix and a categorical grouping variable with 999 permutations.

promptTemplates.title: Build Phylogenetic Tree
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use scikit-bio to build a neighbor-joining tree from a distance matrix, root it at the midpoint, and calculate cophenetic distances between all tips.

bestPractices: Use generators when reading large sequence files to minimize memory usage
Validate that phylogenetic tree tip names exactly match OTU identifiers before calculating phylogenetic diversity
Run PERMDISP test alongside PERMANOVA to verify group dispersion homogeneity
antiPatterns: Loading entire multi-gigabyte FASTA files into memory instead of using streaming generators
Providing relative abundance proportions instead of integer counts for diversity calculations
Running PERMANOVA without checking dispersion assumptions using PERMDISP test

faq.question: What file formats does scikit-bio support?
faq.answer: FASTA, FASTQ, GenBank, EMBL, QSeq for sequences. Newick for trees. BIOM format (HDF5 and JSON) for abundance tables. Also supports Clustal, PHYLIP, Stockholm alignments.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with QIIME 2 data?
faq.answer: Yes. Export QIIME 2 artifacts to get BIOM tables, Newick trees, and distance matrices that scikit-bio can read directly.

faq.question: How do I install the scikit-bio library?
faq.answer: Run pip install scikit-bio or conda install -c conda-forge scikit-bio. Requires Python 3.9 or later.

faq.question: What is the difference between weighted and unweighted UniFrac?
faq.answer: Unweighted UniFrac uses presence/absence of taxa. Weighted UniFrac considers abundance differences. Use weighted for quantitative community comparisons.

faq.question: Why do my phylogenetic metrics fail?
faq.answer: Check that tree tip names exactly match feature IDs in your count matrix. Mismatches cause errors. Use tree.shear() to prune mismatched tips.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with sensitive genomic data?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation. Your data stays local when you run code. No data is transmitted or stored by the skill.
